title: buildah from is not working for emulated architectures
body:
**Description**
When using buildah from on an x86_64 host with --arch s390x for example, the run command fails. This happens with all emulated architectures.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Run the following in a container on an x86_64 host using --arch s390x
1.work_container=$(buildah from fedora)
2.buildah run "${work_container}" /bin/bash -c "echo hello"

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:7eaf774f9ce106e2c6ce771804301f91685601eacbf0c69a6ba63eb64761082b": processing tar file(Error: unknown command "/" for "buildah"

Did you mean this?
        rm

): exit status 125
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Error 1 occurred on 31
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
hello
(this is produced by the echo hello)

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**
Using the official image from quay
```
quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
multiple emulated platforms
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2
```
I am also attaching some scripts and a Dockerfile to easily reproduce this problem.
The host system I used was Fedora 39. The following is a list of qemu and podman rpms installed.

qemu-common-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64
qemu-audio-jack-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64

comments.body:
3 Files:
Dockerfile (Dockerfile with basically just the RUN command)
test.sh (test for a single platform running inside the container)
test/test_all_platforms.sh (main test program)
[test_scripts.tar.gz](https://github.com/containers/buildah/files/13940798/test_scripts.tar.gz)
Do you have the qemu-user-static package installed?

Hi Daniel, thanks for looking into this.
Yes qemu-user-static is installed.
Thats the rpm query on the F39 system .

```
rpm -qa |grep static
qemu-user-static-xtensa-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64
qemu-user-static-x86-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64

qemu-user-static-hexagon-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64
qemu-user-static-cris-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64
qemu-user-static-arm-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64
qemu-user-static-alpha-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64
qemu-user-static-aarch64-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64
qemu-user-static-8.1.3-1.fc39.x86_64

```

If the user static is installed then it should work.  Buildah does not do anything to effect this.  When the host kernel sees an executable compiled for a different arch, the kernel is supposed to use the emulation from qemu-user-static to execute the code.

`Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:7eaf774f9ce106e2c6ce771804301f91685601eacbf0c69a6ba63eb64761082b": processing tar file(Error: unknown command "/" for "buildah"` is not an error from `buildah run`.  It looks like some kind of error from "buildah commit" or "buildah from" mixed with the error you'd get if you ran "buildah /".
I suggest you troubleshoot why the "buildah" command appears to have gotten "/" passed as its first non-option argument, and go from there.

Thanks Nalin for pointing this out.
I tried that manually, not using the scripts I attached in the tar file above.
This is the result:
```
mandres@traktor:~$ podman run --arch s390x --privileged --rm -it -v "$PWD:$PWD" --workdir "$PWD" quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2-s390x bash
[root@bcf46df14b67 mandres]# buildah --arch s390x from fedora
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b457520870ff done   | 
Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:b457520870ffb00b89f9b896fb1d925c2a0b65ecc95a879fe21158fdba1bc643": processing tar file(Error: unknown command "/" for "buildah"

Did you mean this?
	rm

): exit status 125
```

The problem occurs already in the command buildah from fedora.
I thought maybe buildah pulls the from architecture, thats why I also tried with --arch, anyway it yields the same result.

I also tried to pull the correct arch by adding the tag, same result.
```
[root@bcf46df14b67 mandres]# buildah from fedora:39-s390x
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:39-s390x...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b457520870ff done   | 
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:b457520870ffb00b89f9b896fb1d925c2a0b65ecc95a879fe21158fdba1bc643": processing tar file(Error: unknown command "/" for "buildah"

Did you mean this?
	rm

): exit status 125
[root@bcf46df14b67 mandres]# arch
s390x
```

@rhatdan can you please take a look again? Do you have an idea why buildah from does not work on emulated platforms even with the original quay buildah image? Am I doing something wrong?

Just to be sure this is not a silly user error I did install a fresh KVM with fedora 39, installed podman and tried to use a Dockerfile with buildah bud, and it fails the same way. So it seems to me all usage of podman/buildah is broken when using it in an emulated  container.
This is the setup shown below.
(kvm x86 fedora39 (podman run qemu s390x(buildah bud --> fails)))

Could you give me the actual commands you are executing. The last comment above is Buildah within a Podman container?

What happens when you execute something like:
$ buildah bud --arch=s390x .

> Could you give me the actual commands you are executing. The last comment above is Buildah within a Podman container?

podman run --arch s390x --privileged --rm -it quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2-s390x  buildah from fedora

> What happens when you execute something like: $ buildah bud --arch=s390x .

This works. Also, the result is a usable s390x container.

@rhatdan could you please take a look and give some advice how to continue here?

Anybody who can shed some light on this problem?
I think this is a pretty strong restriction on using buildah for platforms where usually cross platform development is done.

How about doing:
podman run --privileged --rm -it quay.io/buildah/stable buildah from --arch s390x fedora

This is starting an x86 container so that works.
```
podman run --privileged --rm -it quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2 buildah from --arch s390x fedora
Trying to pull quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.33.2...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 08a20a796ba1 done   | 
Copying blob 8b4d5e79c1d0 done   | 
Copying blob 801d586e727b done   | 
Copying blob 8deae42c5ba7 done   |

Copying blob 718a00fe3212 done   |

Copying blob 5cb71af33ace done   | 
Copying blob 996fc0f77826 done   | 
Copying config e655b3987b done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b457520870ff done   | 
Copying config 50d1b6a056 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
fedora-working-container
```
In the use case I presented I think that is quite a normal straight forward usage. On  an x86 we spin off an emulated container with --arch s390x or armhf... in order to compile some binaries and then pack them in a container. The first container started with podman is the build container also used to build in OCP on Z.  Thats why we use the same in the developer compile environment on an x86 machine. 

So it seems the straight forward use case does not work with podman/buildah, do you agree Dan?
I suspect there is a generic nesting problem using emulated containers?!
@rhatdan

> In the use case I presented I think that is quite a normal straight forward usage. On an x86 we spin off an emulated container with --arch s390x or armhf... in order to compile some binaries and then pack them in a container. The first container started with podman is the build container also used to build in OCP on Z. Thats why we use the same in the developer compile environment on an x86 machine.

I'd recommend using the `--platform` flag for building for foreign architectures, and running a natively-compiled `buildah` binary.  Base images will be pulled with preference matching the `--platform` setting, and an emulated compiler will usually default to producing binaries for the platform that the compiler itself was built for.  It's how we build our own images.

> So it seems the straight forward use case does not work with podman/buildah, do you agree Dan? I suspect there is a generic nesting problem using emulated containers?! @rhatdan

Emulation is very, very good, but it isn't perfect.  Some of that is bugs, yes, but some of that is limitations of how emulation is configured, and some of it comes down to how the emulator interacts with the kernel.
